1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم الم
1.english: Alif, Lam, Meem
1.transliteration: Alif-Laaam-Meeem

1.arabic: أحسب الناس أن يتركوا أن يقولوا آمنا وهم لا يفتنون
1.english: Do the people think that they will be left to say, "We believe" and they will not be tried?
1.transliteration: Ahasiban naasu anyu yutrakooo any yaqoolooo aamannaa wa hum la yuftanoon

1.arabic: ولقد فتنا الذين من قبلهم ۖ فليعلمن الله الذين صدقوا وليعلمن الكاذبين
1.english: But We have certainly tried those before them, and Allah will surely make evident those who are truthful, and He will surely make evident the liars.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad fatannal lazeena min qablihim fala ya'lamannal laahul lazeena sadaqoo wa la ya'lamannal kaazibeen

1.arabic: أم حسب الذين يعملون السيئات أن يسبقونا ۚ ساء ما يحكمون
1.english: Or do those who do evil deeds think they can outrun Us? Evil is what they judge.
1.transliteration: Am hasibal lazeena ya'maloonas sayyiaati any yasbiqoonaa; saaa'a maa yahkumoon

1.arabic: من كان يرجو لقاء الله فإن أجل الله لآت ۚ وهو السميع العليم
1.english: Whoever should hope for the meeting with Allah - indeed, the term decreed by Allah is coming. And He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Man kaana yarjoo liqaaa 'allaahi fa inna ajalal laahi laaat; wa Huwass Sameeul 'Aleem

1.arabic: ومن جاهد فإنما يجاهد لنفسه ۚ إن الله لغني عن العالمين
1.english: And whoever strives only strives for [the benefit of] himself. Indeed, Allah is free from need of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa man jaahada fainnamaa yujaahidu linafsih; innal laaha laghaniyyun 'anil 'aalameen

1.arabic: والذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات لنكفرن عنهم سيئاتهم ولنجزينهم أحسن الذي كانوا يعملون
1.english: And those who believe and do righteous deeds - We will surely remove from them their misdeeds and will surely reward them according to the best of what they used to do.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati lanukaf firanna 'anhum saiyiaatihim wa lanajziyannahum ahsanal lazee kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: ووصينا الإنسان بوالديه حسنا ۖ وإن جاهداك لتشرك بي ما ليس لك به علم فلا تطعهما ۚ إلي مرجعكم فأنبئكم بما كنتم تعملون
1.english: And We have enjoined upon man goodness to parents. But if they endeavor to make you associate with Me that of which you have no knowledge, do not obey them. To Me is your return, and I will inform you about what you used to do.
1.transliteration: Wa wassainal insaana biwaalidaihi husnanw wa in jaahadaaka litushrika bee maa laisa laka bihee 'ilmun falaa tuti'humaa; ilaiya marji'ukum fa unabbi'ukum bimaa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: والذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات لندخلنهم في الصالحين
1.english: And those who believe and do righteous deeds - We will surely admit them among the righteous [into Paradise].
1.transliteration: Wallazeena aamanoo w a'amilus saalihaati lanudkhilan nahum fis saaliheen

1.arabic: ومن الناس من يقول آمنا بالله فإذا أوذي في الله جعل فتنة الناس كعذاب الله ولئن جاء نصر من ربك ليقولن إنا كنا معكم ۚ أوليس الله بأعلم بما في صدور العالمين
1.english: And of the people are some who say, "We believe in Allah," but when one [of them] is harmed for [the cause of] Allah, they consider the trial of the people as [if it were] the punishment of Allah. But if victory comes from your Lord, they say, "Indeed, We were with you." Is not Allah most knowing of what is within the breasts of all creatures?
1.transliteration: Wa minan naasi many yaqoolu aamannaa billaahi faizaaa ooziya fil laahi ja'ala fitnatan naasi ka'azaabil laahi wa la'in jaaa'a nasrum mir Rabbika la yaqoolunna innaa kunnaa ma'akum; awa laisal laahu bi a'lama bimaa fee surdooril 'aalameen

1.arabic: وليعلمن الله الذين آمنوا وليعلمن المنافقين
1.english: And Allah will surely make evident those who believe, and He will surely make evident the hypocrites.
1.transliteration: Wa la ya'lamannal laahul lazeena aamanoo wa la ya'lamannal munaafiqeen

1.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا للذين آمنوا اتبعوا سبيلنا ولنحمل خطاياكم وما هم بحاملين من خطاياهم من شيء ۖ إنهم لكاذبون
1.english: And those who disbelieve say to those who believe, "Follow our way, and we will carry your sins." But they will not carry anything of their sins. Indeed, they are liars.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazaeena kafaroo lillazeena aamanut tabi'oo sabeelanaa walnahmil khataayaakum wa maa hum bihaamileena min khataa yaahum min shai'in innahum lakaaziboon

1.arabic: وليحملن أثقالهم وأثقالا مع أثقالهم ۖ وليسألن يوم القيامة عما كانوا يفترون
1.english: But they will surely carry their [own] burdens and [other] burdens along with their burdens, and they will surely be questioned on the Day of Resurrection about what they used to invent.
1.transliteration: Wa la yahmilunna asqaa lahum wa asqaalam ma'a asqaalihim wa la yus'alunna Yawmal Qiyaamati 'ammaa kaanoo yaftaroon

1.arabic: ولقد أرسلنا نوحا إلى قومه فلبث فيهم ألف سنة إلا خمسين عاما فأخذهم الطوفان وهم ظالمون
1.english: And We certainly sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them a thousand years minus fifty years, and the flood seized them while they were wrongdoers.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad arsalnaa Noohan ilaa qawmihee falabisa feehim alfa sanatin illaa khamseena 'aaman fa akhazahumut toofaanu wa hum zaalimoon

1.arabic: فأنجيناه وأصحاب السفينة وجعلناها آية للعالمين
1.english: But We saved him and the companions of the ship, and We made it a sign for the worlds.
1.transliteration: Fa anjainaahu wa as haabas safeenati wa ja'alnaahaaa Aayatal lil'aalameen

1.arabic: وإبراهيم إذ قال لقومه اعبدوا الله واتقوه ۖ ذلكم خير لكم إن كنتم تعلمون
1.english: And [We sent] Abraham, when he said to his people, "Worship Allah and fear Him. That is best for you, if you should know.
1.transliteration: Wa Ibraheema iz qaala liqawmihi' budul laaha wattaqoohu zaalikum khayrul lakum in kuntum ta'lamoon

1.arabic: إنما تعبدون من دون الله أوثانا وتخلقون إفكا ۚ إن الذين تعبدون من دون الله لا يملكون لكم رزقا فابتغوا عند الله الرزق واعبدوه واشكروا له ۖ إليه ترجعون
1.english: You only worship, besides Allah, idols, and you produce a falsehood. Indeed, those you worship besides Allah do not possess for you [the power of] provision. So seek from Allah provision and worship Him and be grateful to Him. To Him you will be returned."
1.transliteration: Innamaa ta'ubdoona min doonil laahi awsaananw-wa takhluqoona ifkaa; innal lazeena ta'budoona min doonil laahi laa yamlikoona lakum rizqan fabtaghoo 'indal laahir rizqa fabtaghoo 'indal laahir rizqa wa'budoohu washkuroo lahooo ilaihi turja'oon

1.arabic: وإن تكذبوا فقد كذب أمم من قبلكم ۖ وما على الرسول إلا البلاغ المبين
1.english: And if you [people] deny [the message] - already nations before you have denied. And there is not upon the Messenger except [the duty of] clear notification.
1.transliteration: Wa in tukazziboo faqad kazzaba umamum min qablikum wa maa'alar Rasooli illal balaaghul mubeen

1.arabic: أولم يروا كيف يبدئ الله الخلق ثم يعيده ۚ إن ذلك على الله يسير
1.english: Have they not considered how Allah begins creation and then repeats it? Indeed that, for Allah, is easy.
1.transliteration: Awa lam yaraw kaifa yubdi'ul laahul khalqa summa yu'eeduh; inna zaalika 'alal laahi yaseer

1.arabic: قل سيروا في الأرض فانظروا كيف بدأ الخلق ۚ ثم الله ينشئ النشأة الآخرة ۚ إن الله على كل شيء قدير
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "Travel through the land and observe how He began creation. Then Allah will produce the final creation. Indeed Allah, over all things, is competent."
1.transliteration: Qul seeroo fil ardi fanzuroo kaifa baa al khalqa summal laahu yunshi''un nash atal Aakhirah; innal laaha 'alaa kulli shai'in Qadeer

1.arabic: يعذب من يشاء ويرحم من يشاء ۖ وإليه تقلبون
1.english: He punishes whom He wills and has mercy upon whom He wills, and to Him you will be returned.
1.transliteration: Yu'azzibu many yashaaa'u wa yarhamu many yashaaa'; wa ilaihi tuqlaboon

1.arabic: وما أنتم بمعجزين في الأرض ولا في السماء ۖ وما لكم من دون الله من ولي ولا نصير
1.english: And you will not cause failure [to Allah] upon the earth or in the heaven. And you have not other than Allah any protector or any helper.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa antum bimu'jizeena fil ardi wa laa fissamaaa'i wa maa lakum min doonil laahi minw waliyyinw wa laa naseer

1.arabic: والذين كفروا بآيات الله ولقائه أولئك يئسوا من رحمتي وأولئك لهم عذاب أليم
1.english: And the ones who disbelieve in the signs of Allah and the meeting with Him - those have despaired of My mercy, and they will have a painful punishment.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena kafaroo bi Aayaatil laahi wa liqaaa'iheee ulaaa'ika ya'isoo mir rahmatee wa ulaaa'ika lahum 'azaabun aleem

1.arabic: فما كان جواب قومه إلا أن قالوا اقتلوه أو حرقوه فأنجاه الله من النار ۚ إن في ذلك لآيات لقوم يؤمنون
1.english: And the answer of Abraham's people was not but that they said, "Kill him or burn him," but Allah saved him from the fire. Indeed in that are signs for a people who believe.
1.transliteration: Famaa kaana jawaaba qawmiheee illaaa an qaaluqtuloohu aw harriqoohu faanjaahul laahu minan naar; inna fee zaalika la Aayaatil laqawminy yu'minoon

1.arabic: وقال إنما اتخذتم من دون الله أوثانا مودة بينكم في الحياة الدنيا ۖ ثم يوم القيامة يكفر بعضكم ببعض ويلعن بعضكم بعضا ومأواكم النار وما لكم من ناصرين
1.english: And [Abraham] said, "You have only taken, other than Allah, idols as [a bond of] affection among you in worldly life. Then on the Day of Resurrection you will deny one another and curse one another, and your refuge will be the Fire, and you will not have any helpers."
1.transliteration: Wa qaala innamat takhaz tum min doonil laahi awsaanam mawaddata bainikum fil hayaatid dunyaa summa yawmal qiyaamati yakfuru ba'dukum biba 'dinw wa yal'anu ba'dukum ba'danw-wa yal'anu ba'dukum ba'danw wa maa waakumun Naaru wa maa lakum min naasireen

1.arabic: ۞ فآمن له لوط ۘ وقال إني مهاجر إلى ربي ۖ إنه هو العزيز الحكيم
1.english: And Lot believed him. [Abraham] said, "Indeed, I will emigrate to [the service of] my Lord. Indeed, He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise."
1.transliteration: Fa aamana lahoo Loot; wa qaala innee mauhajirun ilaa Rabbee innahoo Huwal 'Azeezul Hakeem

1.arabic: ووهبنا له إسحاق ويعقوب وجعلنا في ذريته النبوة والكتاب وآتيناه أجره في الدنيا ۖ وإنه في الآخرة لمن الصالحين
1.english: And We gave to Him Isaac and Jacob and placed in his descendants prophethood and scripture. And We gave him his reward in this world, and indeed, he is in the Hereafter among the righteous.
1.transliteration: Wa wahabnaa lahoo Ishaaqa wa Ya'Qooba wa ja'alnaa fee zurriyyatihin Nubuwwata wal Kitaaba wa aatainaahu ajrahoo fid dunyaa wa innahoo fil aakhirati laminas saaliheen

1.arabic: ولوطا إذ قال لقومه إنكم لتأتون الفاحشة ما سبقكم بها من أحد من العالمين
1.english: And [mention] Lot, when he said to his people, "Indeed, you commit such immorality as no one has preceded you with from among the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa Lootan iz qaala liqawmiheee innakum laatoonal faahishata maa sabaqakum bihaa min ahadim minal 'aalameen

1.arabic: أئنكم لتأتون الرجال وتقطعون السبيل وتأتون في ناديكم المنكر ۖ فما كان جواب قومه إلا أن قالوا ائتنا بعذاب الله إن كنت من الصادقين
1.english: Indeed, you approach men and obstruct the road and commit in your meetings [every] evil." And the answer of his people was not but they said, "Bring us the punishment of Allah, if you should be of the truthful."
1.transliteration: A'innakum lataatoonar rijaala wa taqta'oonas sabeela wa taatoona fee naadekumul munkara famaa kaana jawaaba qawmiheee illaaa an qaalu' tinaaa bi'azaabil laahi in kunta minas saadiqeen

1.arabic: قال رب انصرني على القوم المفسدين
1.english: He said, "My Lord, support me against the corrupting people."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbin surnee 'alal qawmil mufsideen

1.arabic: ولما جاءت رسلنا إبراهيم بالبشرى قالوا إنا مهلكو أهل هذه القرية ۖ إن أهلها كانوا ظالمين
1.english: And when Our messengers came to Abraham with the good tidings, they said, "Indeed, we will destroy the people of that Lot's city. Indeed, its people have been wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Wa lammaa jaaa'at Rusulunaaa Ibraaheema bil bushraa qaalooo innaa muhlikoo ahli haazihil qaryati inna ahlahaa kaanoo zaalimeen

1.arabic: قال إن فيها لوطا ۚ قالوا نحن أعلم بمن فيها ۖ لننجينه وأهله إلا امرأته كانت من الغابرين
1.english: [Abraham] said, "Indeed, within it is Lot." They said, "We are more knowing of who is within it. We will surely save him and his family, except his wife. She is to be of those who remain behind."
1.transliteration: Qaala inna feeha Lootaa; qaaloo nahnu a'lamu biman feehaa lanunajjjiyannahoo wa ahlahooo illam ra atahoo kaanat minal ghaabireen

1.arabic: ولما أن جاءت رسلنا لوطا سيء بهم وضاق بهم ذرعا وقالوا لا تخف ولا تحزن ۖ إنا منجوك وأهلك إلا امرأتك كانت من الغابرين
1.english: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed for them and felt for them great discomfort. They said, "Fear not, nor grieve. Indeed, we will save you and your family, except your wife; she is to be of those who remain behind.
1.transliteration: Wa lammaaa an jaaa'at Rusulunaa Lootan seee'a bihim wa daaqa bihim zar'anw wa qaaloo laa takhaf wa laa tahzan innaa munajjooka wa ahlaka illam ra ataka kaanat minal ghaabireen

1.arabic: إنا منزلون على أهل هذه القرية رجزا من السماء بما كانوا يفسقون
1.english: Indeed, we will bring down on the people of this city punishment from the sky because they have been defiantly disobedient."
1.transliteration: Innaa munziloona 'alaaa ahli haazihil qaryati rijzam minas samaaa'i bimaa kaanoo yafsuqoon

1.arabic: ولقد تركنا منها آية بينة لقوم يعقلون
1.english: And We have certainly left of it a sign as clear evidence for a people who use reason.
1.transliteration: Wa laqat taraknaa min haaa aayatam baiyinatal liqawminy ya'qiloon

1.arabic: وإلى مدين أخاهم شعيبا فقال يا قوم اعبدوا الله وارجوا اليوم الآخر ولا تعثوا في الأرض مفسدين
1.english: And to Madyan [We sent] their brother Shu'ayb, and he said, "O my people, worship Allah and expect the Last Day and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption."
1.transliteration: Wa ilaa Madyana akhaahum Shu'ayban faqaala yaa qawmi'-budul laaha warjul yawmal aakhira wa laa ta'saw fil ardi mufsideen

1.arabic: فكذبوه فأخذتهم الرجفة فأصبحوا في دارهم جاثمين
1.english: But they denied him, so the earthquake seized them, and they became within their home [corpses] fallen prone.
1.transliteration: Fakazzaboohu fa akhazat humur rajfatu fa asbahoo fee daarihim jaasimeen

1.arabic: وعادا وثمود وقد تبين لكم من مساكنهم ۖ وزين لهم الشيطان أعمالهم فصدهم عن السبيل وكانوا مستبصرين
1.english: And [We destroyed] 'Aad and Thamud, and it has become clear to you from their [ruined] dwellings. And Satan had made pleasing to them their deeds and averted them from the path, and they were endowed with perception.
1.transliteration: Wa 'Aadanw wa Samooda wa qat tabaiyana lakum mim masaakinihim wa zaiyana lahumush Shaitaanu a'maalahum fasaddahum 'anis sabeeli wa kaanoo mustabsireen

1.arabic: وقارون وفرعون وهامان ۖ ولقد جاءهم موسى بالبينات فاستكبروا في الأرض وما كانوا سابقين
1.english: And [We destroyed] Qarun and Pharaoh and Haman. And Moses had already come to them with clear evidences, and they were arrogant in the land, but they were not outrunners [of Our punishment].
1.transliteration: Wa Qaaroona wa Fir'awna wa haamaana wa laqad jaaa'ahum Moosa bilbaiyinaati fastakbaroo fil ardi wa maa kaanoo saabiqeen

1.arabic: فكلا أخذنا بذنبه ۖ فمنهم من أرسلنا عليه حاصبا ومنهم من أخذته الصيحة ومنهم من خسفنا به الأرض ومنهم من أغرقنا ۚ وما كان الله ليظلمهم ولكن كانوا أنفسهم يظلمون
1.english: So each We seized for his sin; and among them were those upon whom We sent a storm of stones, and among them were those who were seized by the blast [from the sky], and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow, and among them were those whom We drowned. And Allah would not have wronged them, but it was they who were wronging themselves.
1.transliteration: Fakullan akhaznaa bizam bihee faminhum man arsalnaa 'alaihi haasibaa; wa minhum man akhazat hus saihatu wa minhum man khasafnaa bihil arda wa minhum man aghraqnaa; wa maa kaanal laahu li yazlimahum wa laakin kaanoo anfusahum yazlimoon

1.arabic: مثل الذين اتخذوا من دون الله أولياء كمثل العنكبوت اتخذت بيتا ۖ وإن أوهن البيوت لبيت العنكبوت ۖ لو كانوا يعلمون
1.english: The example of those who take allies other than Allah is like that of the spider who takes a home. And indeed, the weakest of homes is the home of the spider, if they only knew.
1.transliteration: Masalul lazeenat takhazoo min doonil laahi awliyaaa'a kamasalil 'ankaboot, ittakhazat baitaa; wa inna awhanal buyooti la baitul 'ankaboot; law kaanoo ya'lamoon

1.arabic: إن الله يعلم ما يدعون من دونه من شيء ۚ وهو العزيز الحكيم
1.english: Indeed, Allah knows whatever thing they call upon other than Him. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise.
1.transliteration: Innal laaha ya'lamu maa yad'oona min doonihee min shai'; wa Huwal 'Azeezul Hakeem

1.arabic: وتلك الأمثال نضربها للناس ۖ وما يعقلها إلا العالمون
1.english: And these examples We present to the people, but none will understand them except those of knowledge.
1.transliteration: Wa tilkal amsaalu nadribuhaa linnaasi wa maa ya'qiluhaaa illal 'aalimoon

1.arabic: خلق الله السماوات والأرض بالحق ۚ إن في ذلك لآية للمؤمنين
1.english: Allah created the heavens and the earth in truth. Indeed in that is a sign for the believers.
1.transliteration: Khalaqal laahus samaawaati wal arda bilhaqq; inna fee zaalika la aayatal lilmu mineen

1.arabic: اتل ما أوحي إليك من الكتاب وأقم الصلاة ۖ إن الصلاة تنهى عن الفحشاء والمنكر ۗ ولذكر الله أكبر ۗ والله يعلم ما تصنعون
1.english: Recite, [O Muhammad], what has been revealed to you of the Book and establish prayer. Indeed, prayer prohibits immorality and wrongdoing, and the remembrance of Allah is greater. And Allah knows that which you do.
1.transliteration: Utlu maaa oohiya ilaika mional Kitaabi wa aqimis Salaata innas Salaata tanhaa 'anil fahshaaa'i wal munkar; wa lazikrul laahi akbar; wal laahu ya'lamu maa tasna'oon

1.arabic: ۞ ولا تجادلوا أهل الكتاب إلا بالتي هي أحسن إلا الذين ظلموا منهم ۖ وقولوا آمنا بالذي أنزل إلينا وأنزل إليكم وإلهنا وإلهكم واحد ونحن له مسلمون
1.english: And do not argue with the People of the Scripture except in a way that is best, except for those who commit injustice among them, and say, "We believe in that which has been revealed to us and revealed to you. And our God and your God is one; and we are Muslims [in submission] to Him."
1.transliteration: Wa laa tujaadilooo Ahlal Kitaabi illaa billatee hiya ahsanu illal lazeena zalamoo minhum wa qoolooo aamannaa billazeee unzila ilainaa wa unzila ilaikum wa illaahunna wa illahukum waahidunw-wa nahnu lahoo muslimoon

1.arabic: وكذلك أنزلنا إليك الكتاب ۚ فالذين آتيناهم الكتاب يؤمنون به ۖ ومن هؤلاء من يؤمن به ۚ وما يجحد بآياتنا إلا الكافرون
1.english: And thus We have sent down to you the Qur'an. And those to whom We [previously] gave the Scripture believe in it. And among these [people of Makkah] are those who believe in it. And none reject Our verses except the disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Wa kazaalika anzalnaaa ilaikal Kitaab; fallazeena aatainaahumul kitaaba yu'minoona bihee wa min haaa'ulaaa'i many yu'minu bih; wa maa yajhadu bi'Aayaatinaa illal kaafiroon

1.arabic: وما كنت تتلو من قبله من كتاب ولا تخطه بيمينك ۖ إذا لارتاب المبطلون
1.english: And you did not recite before it any scripture, nor did you inscribe one with your right hand. Otherwise the falsifiers would have had [cause for] doubt.
1.transliteration: Wa maa kunta tatloo min qablihee min kitaabinw wa laa takhuttubhoo bi yameenika izal lartaabal mubtiloon

1.arabic: بل هو آيات بينات في صدور الذين أوتوا العلم ۚ وما يجحد بآياتنا إلا الظالمون
1.english: Rather, the Qur'an is distinct verses [preserved] within the breasts of those who have been given knowledge. And none reject Our verses except the wrongdoers.
1.transliteration: Bal huwa aayaatum baiyinaatun fee sudooril lazeena ootul 'ilm; wa maa yajhadu bi aayaatinaa illaz zaalimoon

1.arabic: وقالوا لولا أنزل عليه آيات من ربه ۖ قل إنما الآيات عند الله وإنما أنا نذير مبين
1.english: But they say, "Why are not signs sent down to him from his Lord?" Say, "The signs are only with Allah, and I am only a clear warner."
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo law laaa unzila 'alaihi aayaatum mir Rabbihee qul innamal aayaatu 'indal laahi wa innamaaa ana nazeerum mubeen

1.arabic: أولم يكفهم أنا أنزلنا عليك الكتاب يتلى عليهم ۚ إن في ذلك لرحمة وذكرى لقوم يؤمنون
1.english: And is it not sufficient for them that We revealed to you the Book which is recited to them? Indeed in that is a mercy and reminder for a people who believe.
1.transliteration: Awa lam yakfihim annaaa anzalnaa 'alaikal kitaaba yutlaa 'alaikhim; inna fee zaalika larahmatanw wa zikraa liqawminy yu'minoon

1.arabic: قل كفى بالله بيني وبينكم شهيدا ۖ يعلم ما في السماوات والأرض ۗ والذين آمنوا بالباطل وكفروا بالله أولئك هم الخاسرون
1.english: Say, "Sufficient is Allah between me and you as Witness. He knows what is in the heavens and earth. And they who have believed in falsehood and disbelieved in Allah - it is those who are the losers."
1.transliteration: Qul kafaa billaahi bainee wa bainakum shaheedaa; ya'lamu maa fis samaawaati wal ard; wallazeena aamanoo bil baatili wa kafaroo billaahi ulaaa'ika humul khaasiroon

1.arabic: ويستعجلونك بالعذاب ۚ ولولا أجل مسمى لجاءهم العذاب وليأتينهم بغتة وهم لا يشعرون
1.english: And they urge you to hasten the punishment. And if not for [the decree of] a specified term, punishment would have reached them. But it will surely come to them suddenly while they perceive not.
1.transliteration: Wa yasta'jiloonaka bil'azaab; wa law laaa ajalum musammal lajaaa'ahumul 'zaab; wa la yaatiyannahum baghta tanw wa hum laa yash'uroon

1.arabic: يستعجلونك بالعذاب وإن جهنم لمحيطة بالكافرين
1.english: They urge you to hasten the punishment. And indeed, Hell will be encompassing of the disbelievers
1.transliteration: Yasta'jiloonak bil'azaab; wa inna Jahannama lamuhee tatum bilkaafireen

1.arabic: يوم يغشاهم العذاب من فوقهم ومن تحت أرجلهم ويقول ذوقوا ما كنتم تعملون
1.english: On the Day the punishment will cover them from above them and from below their feet and it is said, "Taste [the result of] what you used to do."
1.transliteration: Yawma yaghshaahumul 'azaabu min fawqihim wa min tahti arjulim wa yaqoolu zooqoo maa kuntum ta'maloon

1.arabic: يا عبادي الذين آمنوا إن أرضي واسعة فإياي فاعبدون
1.english: O My servants who have believed, indeed My earth is spacious, so worship only Me.
1.transliteration: Yaa 'ibaadiyal lazeena aamanooo inna ardee waasi 'atun fa iyyaaya fa'budoon

1.arabic: كل نفس ذائقة الموت ۖ ثم إلينا ترجعون
1.english: Every soul will taste death. Then to Us will you be returned.
1.transliteration: Kullu nafsin zaaa'iqatul mawti summa ilainaa turja'oon

1.arabic: والذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات لنبوئنهم من الجنة غرفا تجري من تحتها الأنهار خالدين فيها ۚ نعم أجر العاملين
1.english: And those who have believed and done righteous deeds - We will surely assign to them of Paradise [elevated] chambers beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally. Excellent is the reward of the [righteous] workers
1.transliteration: Wallazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati la nubawwi 'annahum minal Jannati ghurafan tajree min tahtihal anhaaru khaalideena feehaa; ni'ma ajrul 'aamileen

1.arabic: الذين صبروا وعلى ربهم يتوكلون
1.english: Who have been patient and upon their Lord rely.
1.transliteration: Allazeena sabaroo wa 'alaa Rabbihim yatawakkaloon

1.arabic: وكأين من دابة لا تحمل رزقها الله يرزقها وإياكم ۚ وهو السميع العليم
1.english: And how many a creature carries not its [own] provision. Allah provides for it and for you. And He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Wa ka ayyim min daaabbatil laa tahmilu riqqahaa; al laahu yarzuquhaa wa iyyaakum; wa Huwas Samee'ul Aleem

1.arabic: ولئن سألتهم من خلق السماوات والأرض وسخر الشمس والقمر ليقولن الله ۖ فأنى يؤفكون
1.english: If you asked them, "Who created the heavens and earth and subjected the sun and the moon?" they would surely say, "Allah." Then how are they deluded?
1.transliteration: Wa la'in sa altahum man khalaqas samaawaati wal arda wa sakhkharash shamsa wal qamara la yaqoolunnal laahu fa ann yu'fakoon

1.arabic: الله يبسط الرزق لمن يشاء من عباده ويقدر له ۚ إن الله بكل شيء عليم
1.english: Allah extends provision for whom He wills of His servants and restricts for him. Indeed Allah is, of all things, Knowing.
1.transliteration: Allaahu yabsutur rizqa limany yashaaa'u min 'ibaadihee wa yaqdiru lah; innal laaha bikulli shai'in Aleem

1.arabic: ولئن سألتهم من نزل من السماء ماء فأحيا به الأرض من بعد موتها ليقولن الله ۚ قل الحمد لله ۚ بل أكثرهم لا يعقلون
1.english: And if you asked them, "Who sends down rain from the sky and gives life thereby to the earth after its lifelessness?" they would surely say " Allah." Say, "Praise to Allah "; but most of them do not reason.
1.transliteration: Wa la'in sa altahum man nazzala minas samaaa'e maaa'an fa ahyaa bihil arda mim ba'di mawtihaa la yaqoolunnal laah; qulil hamdu lillah; bal aksaruhum laa ya'qiloon

1.arabic: وما هذه الحياة الدنيا إلا لهو ولعب ۚ وإن الدار الآخرة لهي الحيوان ۚ لو كانوا يعلمون
1.english: And this worldly life is not but diversion and amusement. And indeed, the home of the Hereafter - that is the [eternal] life, if only they knew.
1.transliteration: Wa maa haazihil hayaa tud dunyaaa illaa lahwunw-wa la'ib; wa innad Daaral Aakhirata la hiyal ha yawaan; law kaano ya'lamoon

1.arabic: فإذا ركبوا في الفلك دعوا الله مخلصين له الدين فلما نجاهم إلى البر إذا هم يشركون
1.english: And when they board a ship, they supplicate Allah, sincere to Him in religion. But when He delivers them to the land, at once they associate others with Him
1.transliteration: Fa-izaa rakiboo fil fulki da'awul laaha mukhilseena lahud deena falammaa najjaa hum ilal baari izaa hum yushrikoon

1.arabic: ليكفروا بما آتيناهم وليتمتعوا ۖ فسوف يعلمون
1.english: So that they will deny what We have granted them, and they will enjoy themselves. But they are going to know.
1.transliteration: Li yakfuroo bimaaa aatainaahum wa li yatamatta'oo fasaw fa ya'lamoon

1.arabic: أولم يروا أنا جعلنا حرما آمنا ويتخطف الناس من حولهم ۚ أفبالباطل يؤمنون وبنعمة الله يكفرون
1.english: Have they not seen that We made [Makkah] a safe sanctuary, while people are being taken away all around them? Then in falsehood do they believe, and in the favor of Allah they disbelieve?
1.transliteration: Awalam yaraw annaa ja'alnaa haraman aaminanw wa yutakhattafun naasu min haw lihim; afabil baatili yu'minoona wa bini'matil laahi yakfuroon

1.arabic: ومن أظلم ممن افترى على الله كذبا أو كذب بالحق لما جاءه ۚ أليس في جهنم مثوى للكافرين
1.english: And who is more unjust than one who invents a lie about Allah or denies the truth when it has come to him? Is there not in Hell a [sufficient] residence for the disbelievers?
1.transliteration: Wa man azlamu mimma nif taraa 'alal laahi kaziban aw kazzaba bilhaqqi lammaa jaaa'ah; alaisa fee jahannama maswal lil kaafireen

1.arabic: والذين جاهدوا فينا لنهدينهم سبلنا ۚ وإن الله لمع المحسنين
1.english: And those who strive for Us - We will surely guide them to Our ways. And indeed, Allah is with the doers of good.
1.transliteration: Wallazeena jaahadoo feenaa lanahdiyannahum subulana; wa innal laaha lama'al muhsineen
